candidates.why: Direct top-line revenue line and most specific reported revenue label.
Also a revenue total and matches the same values; useful as fallback if revenue label changes.
Section header, not the numeric revenue row.

variables.notes: Using a priority list of revenue labels; both are valid revenue rows, with the more specific non-total label first.

candidates.why: Best direct cost-of-sales / cost-of-revenue line.
Equivalent total line with same values; less preferred than the direct operating revenue cost line.
Could be useful conceptually, but standardized COGS is more naturally captured by the reported cost of operating revenue line.

variables.notes: Selected the direct reported cost-of-revenue line, not subtotal alternatives.

candidates.row_label: Selling, General & Administrative Expenses excluding Research & Development Expenses
candidates.why: Explicit SG&A row, populated, and preferred over components/totals per hierarchy.

candidates.row_label: Labor & Related Expenses including Stock-Based Compensation in Selling, General & Administrative Expenses
candidates.why: Overhead component within SG&A, but should not be used when an explicit SG&A row exists and is populated.
Too broad and includes COGS; not suitable when explicit SG&A exists.

final_choice.row_label: Selling, General & Administrative Expenses excluding Research & Development Expenses

variables.notes: Explicit SG&A row exists and is populated, so selected only that row. It explicitly excludes R&D, so no separate XRD add-back into XSGA is needed.

candidates.row_label: Amortization of Capitalized Research & Development Expenses - Supplemental
candidates.why: R&D-related wording exists, but this is amortization below EBIT/supplemental rather than a dedicated current-period R&D expense line.

candidates.row_label: Selling, General & Administrative Expenses excluding Research & Development Expenses
candidates.why: This explicitly excludes R&D and is not itself an R&D expense line.
Balance sheet asset, not income statement R&D expense.

variables.notes: No suitable dedicated R&D expense line appears on the income statement. The only R&D-related income-statement row is amortization of capitalized R&D, which should not be used as XRD.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense - Net of Capitalized Interest
candidates.why: Best recurring interest expense line and more appropriate than totals or EBIT/EBITDA metrics.
Also a valid interest expense line, but likely overlapping with the net-of-capitalized-interest line; avoid double counting.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supple
candidates.why: Recurring finance cost component, but may already be included in interest expense; selecting separately risks double counting.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense - Net of Capitalized Interest

variables.notes: Selected the most granular primary recurring interest expense row and avoided combining overlapping interest rows.

candidates.row_label: Shareholders' Equity - Attributable to Parent Shareholders - Total
candidates.why: Best book equity line because it is explicitly attributable to parent and excludes non-controlling interests.

candidates.row_label: Common Equity Attributable to Parent Shareholders
candidates.why: Also a valid parent-only equity line with identical values; useful as fallback.
Appears to match parent common equity; acceptable fallback.

candidates.row_label: Total Shareholders' Equity - including Minority Interest & Hybrid Debt
candidates.why: Too broad and should be avoided when parent-attributable equity exists.

final_choice.row_label: Shareholders' Equity - Attributable to Parent Shareholders - Total
Common Equity Attributable to Parent Shareholders

variables.notes: Using a priority list of parent-attributable/common equity rows to capture book equity while excluding minority interest.

candidates.row_label: Total Shareholders' Equity - including Minority Interest & Hybrid Debt
candidates.why: Could indicate inclusion of minority interest, but no separate non-controlling interest line exists and this row equals parent equity values.
Income statement line, not a balance sheet minority interest/equity line.
Income statement line, not a balance sheet minority interest/equity line.

variables.notes: No separate minority interest / non-controlling interest line is present on the balance sheet. BE uses the parent-attributable equity line, so MIB should be zero.
